execute_steps: run: cd src-electron/runtime && npx tsx --test test/plugin-registry.test.ts
create fixture dir with valid package.json + xyzAgent field
call registry.scan([dir]) → assert result.length === 1
assert result[0].pluginId === dir name
assert result[0].status === 'UNLOADED' (internal state)
run: cd src-electron/runtime && npx tsx --test test/plugin-registry.test.ts
create fixture dir with package.json missing xyzAgent
call registry.scan([dir]) → assert result.length === 0
console output: 'missing or invalid xyzAgent manifest, skipping'
run: cd src-electron/runtime && npx tsx --test test/plugin-registry.test.ts
create plugin with contributes.slashCommands: [{name:'hello'}]
call registry.scan() → assert activationEvents includes 'onSlashCommand:hello'
verified: tools/hooks inference also works (added in dev phase)
run: cd src-electron/runtime && npx tsx --test test/plugin-host.test.ts
call host.assignWorker('plugin-a','sandbox') → get workerId A
call host.assignWorker('plugin-b','sandbox') → get workerId B
assert A !== B (exclusive workers for sandbox)
run: cd src-electron/runtime && npx tsx --test test/plugin-host.test.ts
call host.assignWorker('plugin-a','trusted') → get workerId A
call host.assignWorker('plugin-b','trusted') → get workerId B
assert A === B (shared worker for trusted)
run: cd src-electron/runtime && npx tsx --test test/plugin-host.test.ts
assign sandbox worker, set crash callback
trigger worker error via port.postMessage
assert crash callback invoked with (workerId, pluginIds, error)
test_execution.evidence: plugin-host.test.ts 'crash callback is invoked when worker errors' passed (201.82ms)

execute_steps: run: cd src-electron/runtime && npx tsx --test test/plugin-rpc.test.ts
register 'test.echo' handler on PluginRpcServer
dispatch RPC request → worker receives { type: 'rpc', response: { jsonrpc:'2.0', id, result } }
assert response.result deep equals handler return value
run: cd src-electron/runtime && npx tsx --test test/plugin-rpc-client.test.ts
create PluginRpcClient, attach mock port
call client.request('test.slow', {}, 50) — 50ms timeout
do NOT send response (simulate unresponsive server)
await promise → catch error
assert elapsed >= 45ms (real timeout)
assert error.message includes 'RPC timeout'
assert error.code === -32000 (PluginRpcErrorCodes.RPC_TIMEOUT)
test_execution.evidence: plugin-rpc-client.test.ts TC-3-02 passed (52.46ms — real timeout wait confirmed)

execute_steps: run: cd src-electron/runtime && npx tsx --test test/plugin-rpc-client.test.ts
send 3 concurrent requests with different params
respond out of order (C, A, B)
assert Promise.all resolves to ['A', 'B', 'C'] — correct id mapping
test_execution.evidence: plugin-rpc-client.test.ts 'concurrent requests receive correct responses by id' passed (0.19ms)

execute_steps: run: cd src-electron/runtime && npx tsx --test test/plugin-activator.test.ts
TC-4-01: registerDescriptors with plugin having activationEvents: ['onStartupFinished']
assert eventMap maps 'onStartupFinished' → [pluginId]
assert getActivePlugins() returns empty (not yet activated)
run: cd src-electron/runtime && npx tsx --test test/plugin-activator.test.ts
TC-4-02: register plugin with activationEvents: ['onSlashCommand:hello']
call activator.handleEvent({type:'onSlashCommand', command:'hello'})
assert activatePlugin called → state becomes ACTIVE
run: cd src-electron/runtime && npx tsx --test test/plugin-activator.test.ts
TC-4-03: activatePlugin → state becomes ACTIVE
TC-4-04: deactivatePlugin → state becomes UNLOADED
deactivateAll → all active plugins become UNLOADED
run: cd src-electron/runtime && npx tsx --test test/plugin-storage.test.ts
TC-5-01: storage.set('p','k',{value:42}), storage.get('p','k') → assert deep equals {value:42}
TC-5-04: storage.flush('p'), create new PluginStorage with same dir, get('p','k') → persists
run: cd src-electron/runtime && npx tsx --test test/plugin-storage.test.ts
set value exceeding 1MB → assert error with code -32021
set multiple values totaling >10MB → assert error with code -32040
run: cd src-electron/runtime && npx tsx --test test/plugin-integration.test.ts
TC-int-02: two plugins write different keys concurrently through RPC
flush both, verify each plugin's storage file has only its own keys
assert no cross-contamination between plugin A and plugin B
run: cd src-electron && npx vitest run test/protocol-extension.test.ts test/event-adapter-extension.test.ts test/extension-service.test.ts test/server-extension.test.ts
assert all 4 test files pass (66 tests total)
verify no regression from plugin system changes
